title: Making weekend plans
setting: You, Sara, and Lukas are figuring out what to do this weekend.

steps.speaker: Sara
steps.es: Was wänd mir am Wucheänd zäme mache?
steps.en: What shall we do together this weekend?

options.es: Mir chönted uf de Rigi go wandere, bi schööm Wätter.
options.en: We could hike up the Rigi, if the weather's nice.
options.feedback: A concrete, local suggestion with a sensible condition. Great planning.

options.en: Whatever, what do you two want?
options.feedback: Easy-going is fine, but offering an idea moves things forward.
Try to contribute one option — it helps the group decide.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Gueti Idee! Aber s'Wätter söll am Samschtig schlächt sii.
steps.en: Good idea! But the weather's supposed to be bad on Saturday.

options.es: Denn gömmer am Sunntig, und Samschtig is Museum?
options.en: Then let's go Sunday, and Saturday to a museum?
options.feedback: Flexible problem-solving with a plan B. Exactly the right instinct.

options.es: Schade. Denn halt es anders Mal.
options.feedback: Don't give up so fast — a small reshuffle saves the weekend.

options.en: I don't care about the weather.
options.feedback: Hiking the Rigi in bad weather isn't much fun — better to adapt the plan.

steps.speaker: Sara
steps.es: Perfekt! Sunntig Rigi, Samschtig öppis Gmüetlichs.
steps.en: Perfect! Sunday the Rigi, Saturday something cozy.

options.es: Top. Wänn und wo treffed mir öis am Sunntig?
options.en: Great. When and where do we meet on Sunday?
options.feedback: Nailing down the details — that's how plans actually happen.
Enthusiastic and positive. You could pin the time too.
Vague after a plan came together — lock in the time so it's real.

steps.speaker: Sara
steps.es: Am nüni am Bahnhof. Ich freu mich uf öis drü!
steps.en: At nine at the station. I'm looking forward to the three of us!

options.es: Ich au! Bis Sunntig denn, ihr zwöi.
options.en: Me too! See you Sunday then, you two.
options.feedback: Warm and confirming. The plan is set and the mood is great.
'Ja ja' can sound dismissive — a warmer note suits the good mood.
